name: SmoothCamera
previewIconUrl: https://resources.gdevelop-app.com/assets/Icons/Line Hero Pack/Master/SVG/Computers and Hardware/Computers and Hardware_camcoder_gopro_go_pro_camera.svg
shortDescription: Smoothly scroll to follow an object.

eventsBasedBehaviors.description: Smoothly scroll to follow an object.
eventsBasedBehaviors.name: SmoothCamera

eventsFunctions.name: MoveCameraCloser
eventsFunctions.description: Move the camera closer to the object. This action must be called after the object has moved for the frame.

eventsFunctions.name: WaitAndCatchUp
eventsFunctions.description: Delay the camera according to a maximum speed and catch up the delay.

eventsFunctions.name: DrawDebug
eventsFunctions.description: Draw the targeted and actual camera position.

eventsFunctions.name: SetFollowOnX
eventsFunctions.description: Enable or disable the following on X axis.

eventsFunctions.name: SetFollowOnY
eventsFunctions.description: Enable or disable the following on Y axis.

eventsFunctions.name: SetFollowFreeAreaRight
eventsFunctions.description: Change the camera follow free area right border.

eventsFunctions.name: SetFollowFreeAreaLeft
eventsFunctions.description: Change the camera follow free area left border.

eventsFunctions.name: SetFollowFreeAreaTop
eventsFunctions.description: Change the camera follow free area top border.

eventsFunctions.name: SetFollowFreeAreaBottom
eventsFunctions.description: Change the camera follow free area bottom border.

eventsFunctions.name: SetLeftwardSpeedMax
eventsFunctions.description: Change the camera leftward maximum speed (in pixels per second).

eventsFunctions.name: SetRightwardSpeedMax
eventsFunctions.description: Change the camera rightward maximum speed (in pixels per second).

eventsFunctions.name: SetUpwardSpeedMax
eventsFunctions.description: Change the camera upward maximum speed (in pixels per second).

eventsFunctions.name: SetDownwardSpeedMax
eventsFunctions.description: Change the camera downward maximum speed (in pixels per second).

eventsFunctions.name: SetLeftwardSpeed
eventsFunctions.description: Change the camera leftward catch-up speed (in ratio per second).

eventsFunctions.name: SetRightwardSpeed
eventsFunctions.description: Change the camera rightward catch-up speed (in ratio per second).

eventsFunctions.name: SetDownwardSpeed
eventsFunctions.description: Change the camera downward catch-up speed (in ratio per second).

eventsFunctions.name: SetUpwardSpeed
eventsFunctions.description: Change the camera upward catch-up speed (in ratio per second).

eventsFunctions.name: OffsetX
eventsFunctions.description: Compare the camera offset on X axis of the object. This is not the current difference between the object and the camera position.

eventsFunctions.name: SetOffsetXOp
eventsFunctions.description: Change the camera offset on X axis of the object. This is not the current difference between the object and the camera position.

eventsFunctions.name: OffsetY
eventsFunctions.description: Compare the camera offset on Y axis of the object. This is not the current difference between the object and the camera position.

eventsFunctions.name: SetOffsetYOp
eventsFunctions.description: Change the camera offset on Y axis of the object. This is not the current difference between the object and the camera position.

eventsFunctions.name: SetForecastTime
eventsFunctions.description: Change the camera forecast time (in seconds).

eventsFunctions.name: SetCameraDelay
eventsFunctions.description: Change the camera delay (in seconds).

eventsBasedBehaviors.description: Smoothly scroll to follow a character and stabilize the camera when jumping.
eventsBasedBehaviors.name: SmoothPlatformerCamera

description:
The camera follows an object according to:
- a frame rate independent catch-up speed to make the scrolling from smooth to strong
- a maximum speed to do linear following ([open the project online](https://editor.gdevelop.io/?project=example://platformer-with-tilemap)) or slow down the camera when teleporting the object
- a follow-free zone to avoid scrolling on small movements
- an offset to see further in one direction
- an extra delay and catch-up speed to give an impression of speed (useful for dash)
- position forecasting and delay to simulate a cameraman response time

A platformer dedicated behavior allows to switch of settings when the character is in air or on the floor. This can be used to stabilize the camera when jumping.
